request_debug.prompt_preview:
You write per-candidate post-mortems for a slopmortem pipeline. Given a fresh pitch and one dead-startup canonical document, produce a single LLMSynthesis JSON object that explains why this dead startup is similar, where the new pitch could diverge, what killed the dead startup, and what the new founder should learn.

Rules:
- Ground every claim in the candidate document or in tool calls. Do not invent facts.
- A `Trusted facts` block (typed pipeline data — not user input) appears below the pitc

response.text: {"candidate_id":"yo::social_communication","name":"yo","one_liner":"Single-function social notification app that let users send only the word 'Yo' to friends, reaching #1 on the iOS social chart before fading into irrelevance by 2016.","similarity":{"business_model":{"score":4,"rationale":"Yo used tiered_freemium/free monetization with no disclosed ad-supported revenue layer; the new pitch is explicitly ad-supported. Both are free B2C consumer mobile apps targeting viral social behavior, but the revenue engine differs materially."},"market":{"score":7,"rationale":"Both compete in the consumer social/communication mobile app market, targeting young users (Yo was viral in a similar demographic) on iOS/Android globally. The new pitch narrows to college communities, a tighter niche, but the core market — ephemeral, low-friction social interaction among young consumers — is nearly identical."},"gtm":{"score":7,"rationale":"Both rely on organic/viral go-to-market within dense social networks (Yo via Product Hunt and word-of-mouth; new pitch via college towers). Neither uses paid acquisition as a primary channel. Virality within a contained social group is the core growth mechanism in both cases."},"stage_scale":{"score":6,"rationale":"Yo raised ~$2.5M at a $5-10M valuation after a viral spike; it was an early-stage consumer app that peaked quickly and then stagnated. The new pitch appears to be at a similarly early, pre-scale viral moment anchored in a single location type."}},"why_similar":"Both are minimalist, single-mechanic B2C social mobile apps designed for ultra-low-friction interaction (Yo's one-tap notification vs. 30-second location-anchored voice notes). Both pursued viral growth within dense, relationship-rich social clusters rather than broad paid acquisition. Both are free consumer products on iOS/Android targeting young users, and both leaned on novelty as the primary hook. The location-anchoring concept in the new pitch even mirrors Yo's later addition of location-sharing as a feature expansion.","where_diverged":"1. Monetization model: The new pitch is explicitly ad-supported, whereas Yo had no functioning ad revenue model — it was purely free/freemium with no disclosed monetization strategy. 2. Content format: The new pitch uses 30-second voice notes (audio content), giving it richer, repeatable content loops compared to Yo's single-word text/audio ping, which offered no ongoing content value. 3. Location constraint: The new pitch ties posts to a single recurring physical location (e.g., a college tower), creating a place-based community layer absent from Yo. 4. Target community: The new pitch is explicitly college-focused rather than globally undifferentiated. These dimensions give the new pitch more surface area for retention and monetization that Yo lacked.","failure_causes":["no sustainable monetization model","single-mechanic novelty with no content depth","retention collapse after hype cycle ended","product ran on 'autopilot' — founder disengagement","security vulnerability (phone number exposure) damaged trust","inability to defend against clones and parodies","failure to expand beyond gimmick into durable social utility"],"lessons_for_input":["Validate ad revenue yield early — novelty-driven DAUs do not automatically convert to ad CPMs, especially for sub-30-second micro-interactions.","Design for retention from day one: a single recurring mechanic (like Yo's ping or your voice note) must have a content-loop reason to return daily beyond novelty.","Anchor the viral moment to a defensible community (your college-tower niche is stronger than Yo's undifferentiated global launch) — protect and deepen that moat before expanding.","Plan for post-hype plateau: Yo's traffic collapsed when media attention faded with no engagement floor; build habit triggers into the product itself.","Ensure security and trust infrastructure is production-grade before viral scale — Yo's phone-number hack at peak popularity caused lasting credibility damage."]}
